title: King Horn
summary: The repository XML is a U-M 2006 uncorrected-OCR encoding derived from Joseph Hall's 1901 Clarendon edition. Hall prints three separate close manuscript transcriptions of King Horn in parallel: British Library Harley MS 2253, Bodleian MS Laud Misc. 108, and Cambridge University Library MS Gg.4.27(2). The XML serializes those print columns as consecutive complete versions, then encodes Hall's appendix Horn Childe and Maiden Rimnild, a distinct romance transcribed chiefly from the Auchinleck manuscript with a small number of readings supplied or corrected from earlier editors. This graph distinguishes the abstract King Horn work, its three witnesses, Hall's edition, U-M's encoding, the repository artifact, the separate Horn Childe work and witness, prior editions Hall consulted, and later comparison scholarship.
review_notes: The cataloging subject is the anonymous Middle English romance King Horn, not Hall's whole 1901 volume and not one particular manuscript version.
Harley, Laud, and Cambridge are parallel source witnesses. Their line totals and textual differences must not be collapsed into one notional canonical transcription.
Horn Childe and Maiden Rimnild is a separate romance printed and encoded as an appendix; it is related to King Horn but is not a work part or a fourth witness of King Horn.
Hall's manuscript genealogy and dialect localizations are preserved as historical editorial hypotheses, not substituted for current institutional cataloging or asserted as secure filiation.

The complete public Digital Bodleian and Cambridge Digital Library objects are cached as image sets. King Horn occupies Bodleian IIIF canvases 454-471 and CUDL canvases 15-29. The British Library describes a complete digitization of Harley MS 2253, but its images remain unavailable during service recovery.

The U-M XML's 396 PB instances refer to 352 unique page images: 44 references are duplicated because Hall's Harley and Laud columns share printed pages before the encoding serializes them into separate divisions.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for acn1637
entities.description: Repository XML supplied through the pinned CME submodule.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item ACN1637.0001.001
entities.description: A 2006 Level 1 TEI-in-Libraries encoding produced by uncorrected OCR from 352 linked 600 dpi page images. It serializes Hall's three parallel King Horn columns as separate versions, retains notes and typography selectively, and fully encodes the distinct Horn Childe appendix.
entities.reproduction_format: Uncorrected OCR linked to 352 monochrome 600 dpi TIFF G4 page images

entities.label: Hall's 1901 King Horn
entities.description: The immediate print source. Hall prints close transcriptions of all three King Horn witnesses in parallel, expands contractions in italics, corrects only what he regards as obvious scribal errors while normally reporting manuscript readings, punctuates the Cambridge text, and adds introduction, commentary, glossary, indexes, and the distinct Horn Childe appendix.

bibliographic.citation: King Horn: A Middle-English Romance, Edited from the Manuscripts, ed. Joseph Hall (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1901).
bibliographic.title: King Horn: A Middle-English Romance, Edited from the Manuscripts

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Hall's 1901 King Horn
entities.description: A complete 398-page scan used to inspect Hall's title page, introduction, parallel layout, commentary, and Horn Childe appendix.

entities.label: British Library, Harley MS 2253
entities.description: The Harley King Horn witness, item 58, ff. 83r-92v. The current catalog dates the manuscript c. 1330-1340 and localizes its production to the West Midlands, probably Ludlow; Hall's earlier dating and Leominster hypothesis are retained only as historical editorial claims.

date_statements.label: Hall's historical dating
date_statements.notes: Attributed historical editorial estimate; it does not replace the current catalog date.

holding.notes: King Horn is item 58, ff. 83r-92v; Hall's milestones use a/b for recto/verso.

notes: Written by the principal Harley scribe; current catalog localization is West Midlands, probably Ludlow.

entities.label: British Library digital facsimile of Harley MS 2253
entities.description: The exact catalog record states that a complete digital surrogate exists, but the images are not currently available while British Library digital services recover. No image files were cached.
entities.reproduction_format: Complete digital coverage cataloged; current image delivery unavailable

entities.label: Bodleian Library, MS Laud Misc. 108
entities.description: The Laud King Horn witness at ff. 219va-228rb in a manuscript currently dated 1301-1325. Hall treated it as not earlier than about 1310; that historical estimate remains attributed rather than normalized into the current catalog record.

date_statements.label: Hall's historical dating
date_statements.notes: Hall gives no upper bound; his estimate is retained as editorial history rather than merged with the current catalog range.

entities.label: Digital Bodleian facsimile of MS Laud Misc. 108
entities.description: A complete 496-canvas IIIF facsimile of the codex. King Horn runs from fol. 219v, canvas 454, through fol. 228r, canvas 471; its two-column text begins at 219va and ends at 228rb.
entities.reproduction_format: 496 IIIF canvases, including covers and all manuscript surfaces; downloadable JPEGs limited by service to 4000 pixels

entities.label: Cambridge University Library, MS Gg.4.27(2)
entities.description: The Cambridge King Horn witness at ff. 6r-13r in a fourteen-leaf codicological unit, written in one Anglicana hand in two columns. CUDL dates it to the first quarter of the fourteenth century; Hall's much earlier c. 1260 estimate and Anglo-French-scribe hypothesis remain historical claims.

date_statements.label: current catalog date
date_statements.value: first quarter of the fourteenth century

date_statements.label: Hall's historical dating
date_statements.notes: Hall's estimate is contradicted by current cataloging and is retained only as editorial history.

holding.notes: King Horn is manuscript item 2, ff. 6r-13r; the composite manuscript was rebound in 1983.

notes: One Anglicana hand.

entities.label: Cambridge Digital Library facsimile of MS Gg.4.27(2)
entities.description: A complete 40-canvas digital object covering both covers, endleaves, and all fourteen manuscript leaves. King Horn occupies canvases 15-29, corresponding to ff. 6r-13r.
entities.reproduction_format: 40 IIIF canvases; public download images up to 2000 pixels

entities.label: Horn Childe and Maiden Rimnild
entities.description: A distinct anonymous Middle English tail-rhyme romance related to the Horn story, uniquely preserved in the Auchinleck manuscript. Hall prints it as an appendix; the U-M encoding includes it completely but it is outside the cataloging boundary of King Horn.

entities.label: National Library of Scotland, Advocates MS 19.2.1 (Auchinleck manuscript)
entities.description: The unique witness of Horn Childe and Maiden Rimnild, ff. 317va-323vb, copied by Auchinleck Scribe 1. The 1,136 surviving lines are arranged in twelve-line tail-rhyme stanzas; a leaf is lost after fol. 321 and the ending is imperfect after fol. 323.

date_statements.label: Hall's historical dating
date_statements.value: first quarter of the fourteenth century

holding.notes: The Faculty of Advocates shelfmark changed in 1840; the manuscript passed to the National Library of Scotland in 1925. Horn Childe occupies ff. 317va-323vb.

notes: Produced in London in the 1330s; the present codex has losses and separately preserved fragments.

entities.label: Auchinleck Manuscript digital facsimile
entities.description: The NLS-supported Auchinleck project describes a full digital facsimile and exposes individual IIIF Image API services for surviving manuscript leaves and fragments. Horn Childe spans thirteen photographed sides, fol. 317v through fol. 323v.
entities.reproduction_format: Individual IIIF JPEG images and Image Information documents linked from project transcriptions

entities.label: Ritson's Ancient Engleish Metrical Romanceës
entities.description: Ritson's three-volume collection prints the Harley King Horn in volume 2 and Horn Childe in volume 3. Hall cites Ritson for several Horn Childe corrections and for an alternative supplied reading at line 324.

bibliographic.citation: Ancient Engleish Metrical Romanceës, ed. Joseph Ritson, 3 vols. (London: W. Bulmer for G. and W. Nicol, 1802), King Horn in vol. 2, pp. 91-155; Horn Childe in vol. 3, pp. 282-320.
bibliographic.title: Ancient Engleish Metrical Romanceës
bibliographic.publisher: W. Bulmer for G. and W. Nicol

entities.label: Michel's Horn et Rimenhild
entities.description: Michel prints the Cambridge King Horn with variants and Horn Childe. Hall selects Michel's supplied inne at Horn Childe line 324 and otherwise consults the edition historically.

bibliographic.citation: Horn et Rimenhild: Recueil de ce qui reste des poëmes relatifs à leurs aventures, ed. Francisque Michel (Paris: Maulde et Renou for the Bannatyne Club, 1845).
bibliographic.title: Horn et Rimenhild
bibliographic.publisher: Maulde et Renou for the Bannatyne Club

entities.label: Wissmann's Das Lied von King Horn
entities.description: A critical King Horn edition with introduction, notes, glossary, and manuscript variants. Hall explicitly acknowledges Wissmann as especially helpful; it is modeled as consulted, not as a replacement source witness.

bibliographic.citation: Das Lied von King Horn: Mit Einleitung, Anmerkungen und Glossar, ed. Theodor Wissmann, Quellen und Forschungen 45 (Strassburg: Karl J. Trübner, 1881).
bibliographic.title: Das Lied von King Horn: Mit Einleitung, Anmerkungen und Glossar

entities.label: Caro's Horn Childe and Maiden Rimnild
entities.description: Caro's Auchinleck text and study in Englische Studien 12. Hall adopts Caro's supplied readings at Horn Childe lines 20 and 1081.

bibliographic.citation: Georg Caro, 'Kleine Publikationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. X. Horn Childe and Maiden Rimnild,' Englische Studien 12 (1889), 323-366, edited text 351-366.
bibliographic.title: Kleine Publikationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. X. Horn Childe and Maiden Rimnild

entities.label: METS Four Romances of England King Horn edition
entities.description: Herzman, Drake, and Salisbury's later teaching edition uses Cambridge MS Gg.4.27(2) as its base text and draws selected emendations from Laud and Harley. It is a later comparison source and did not feed Hall's 1901 edition or U-M's 2006 encoding.

bibliographic.citation: Four Romances of England: King Horn, Havelok the Dane, Bevis of Hampton, Athelston, ed. Ronald B. Herzman, Graham Drake, and Eve Salisbury (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 1999).
bibliographic.title: Four Romances of England: King Horn, Havelok the Dane, Bevis of Hampton, Athelston

entities.label: U.S. Copyright Office copyright-duration and restoration guidance
entities.description: Current official Circular 15A explains copyright duration, including the maximum 95-year term for pre-1978 statutory protection; Circular 38B explains automatic URAA restoration of eligible foreign works and that a restored work receives only the remainder of the term it would otherwise have enjoyed.

editorial_practices.description: Hall aims at close reproductions, expands contractions in italics, and normally reports the manuscript form in a footnote when correcting an obvious blunder. He explicitly says that he punctuates the Cambridge King Horn text and that Horn Childe follows its manuscript except for punctuation; the same claim is not generalized here to Harley or Laud.
editorial_practices.scope: All three King Horn witness texts and, with separately stated details, Horn Childe.
editorial_practices.source_feature: manuscript spelling, abbreviation, error, and punctuation
editorial_practices.representation: expanded abbreviations; editorial punctuation explicitly for Cambridge and Horn Childe; corrected text with manuscript readings reported

editorial_practices.description: Hall arranges Laud and Harley alongside Cambridge so readers can compare complete versions rather than presenting one synthetic critical text.
editorial_practices.representation: parallel printed columns keyed chiefly to Cambridge commentary

editorial_practices.description: Hall's Horn Childe text is chiefly a close Auchinleck transcription, but it incorporates Caro's supplies at lines 20 and 1081, Michel's inne at line 324, and corrections attributed to Ritson at lines 904, 948, 1051, and 1060.
editorial_practices.source_feature: manuscript omissions and readings judged corrupt
editorial_practices.representation: selected readings supplied or corrected from named prior editors

editorial_practices.description: The encoding was produced by uncorrected OCR with no content editing. It includes the three witness texts, notes embedded with them, and the full Horn Childe appendix, but omits most of Hall's introduction, commentary, glossary, and indexes; it also serializes Hall's parallel columns into consecutive witness divisions.
editorial_practices.source_feature: front matter, edited texts, appendix, commentary, and back matter
editorial_practices.representation: edited texts and appendix encoded; major scholarly apparatus omitted

rights.component: current U-M digital item
rights.basis: The current U-M item applies No Copyright - United States. The embedded 2006 XML availability text is older and generic, so it is preserved as a conflict rather than silently harmonized.

rights.component: availability statement embedded in the 2006 XML header
rights.jurisdiction: Unspecified; University of Michigan provider statement
rights.basis: The embedded availability paragraph says that copyright applies where applicable and that other distribution restrictions may apply, directing readers to U-M for more information. It is a generic conditional notice, not an item-specific license, and is retained separately from the current NoC-US item label.

rights.component: repository XML derived from the U-M item
rights.basis: The repository serialization has no explicit artifact-level license. Its U-M source is currently labeled No Copyright - United States, but the embedded XML also retains a generic historical warning; neither statement is silently transferred into an unconditional license for repository-specific changes.

rights.component: 1901 published edition text
rights.basis: The edition was first published in the United Kingdom in 1901. Even if it qualified for URAA restoration, restored protection lasts only for the remainder of the term it would otherwise have had; the applicable maximum pre-1978 term has expired.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan file and item metadata
rights.basis: The underlying 1901 edition is public domain in the United States, but the item metadata exposes no item-specific license URL for the scan package.

rights.component: medieval manuscript text as historical content, excluding photography and repository services
rights.basis: The medieval textual content is centuries old; this conclusion does not cover modern images, metadata, or access contracts.

rights.component: medieval manuscript text as historical content, excluding photography and repository services
rights.basis: The medieval textual content is centuries old; this conclusion does not cover modern images, metadata, or access contracts.

rights.component: medieval manuscript text as historical content, excluding photography and repository services
rights.basis: The medieval textual content is centuries old; this conclusion does not cover modern images, metadata, or access contracts.

rights.component: medieval manuscript text as historical content, excluding project transcription, photography, and repository services
rights.basis: The medieval textual content is centuries old; this conclusion does not extend to the Auchinleck project's modern transcription, images, essays, or descriptions.

rights.component: British Library digital manuscript images
rights.basis: The images are unavailable and no item-level reuse statement could be inspected. British Library guidance says supplied images may carry copyright and directs users to check licensing information; it does not establish that permission is invariably required for this item.

rights.component: Digital Bodleian manuscript photographs and cached IIIF JPEGs
rights.basis: The IIIF manifest attributes the photography to the Bodleian and applies CC BY-NC 4.0, with Digital Bodleian terms also linked.

rights.component: CUDL images made available for download and cached IIIF JPEGs
rights.basis: The CUDL TEI metadata states that images made available for download are licensed CC BY-NC 4.0; separately, zoom-view images are described as all rights reserved.

rights.component: Auchinleck project images, transcriptions, essays, and descriptions
rights.basis: The project's own citation and reuse page says its texts, essays, images, and descriptions are copyrighted, permits personal use, and requires authorization for commercial use or publication.

rights.component: current NLS permanent digital-object viewer and any files it serves
rights.basis: The current catalog confirms the permanent digital object, but viewer-level rights and downloadable-file terms could not be inspected through the automated-access challenge. Current NLS copyright guidance applies; the older Auchinleck project statement is not silently transferred to this newer service.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan files and item metadata, excluding the public-domain 1802 edition text
rights.basis: The underlying edition is public domain in the United States, but no item-specific open license for the scan files was verified.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan file and item metadata, excluding the public-domain 1845 edition text
rights.basis: The underlying edition is public domain in the United States, but no item-specific open license for the scan file was verified.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan file and item metadata, excluding the public-domain 1881 edition text
rights.basis: The underlying edition is public domain in the United States, but no item-specific open license for the scan file was verified.

rights.component: Internet Archive scan file and item metadata, excluding the public-domain 1889 article text
rights.basis: The underlying article is public domain in the United States, but no item-specific open license for the scan file was verified.

rights.component: 1802 published edition text
rights.basis: The edition was first published in the United Kingdom in 1802. Any possible U.S. statutory or restored term expired long ago; Internet Archive terms still apply to access services.

rights.component: 1845 published edition text
rights.basis: The edition was first published in France in 1845. Any possible U.S. statutory or restored term expired long ago; Internet Archive terms still apply to access services.

rights.component: 1881 published edition text
rights.basis: The edition was first published in Germany in 1881. Any possible U.S. statutory or restored term expired long ago; Internet Archive terms still apply to access services.

rights.component: 1889 published article and edition text
rights.basis: The article was first published in Germany in 1889. Any possible U.S. statutory or restored term expired long ago; Internet Archive terms still apply to access services.

rights.component: 1999 scholarly edition and current downloadable PDF
rights.basis: The modern edition remains presumptively protected; the public reading interface and PDF expose access but no verified open license was found.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/acn1637.xml.
evidence.locator: HEADER; three King Horn version divisions; Horn Childe appendix division
evidence.summary: Identifies U-M item ACN1637.0001.001, Hall's 1901 source edition, uncorrected OCR and Level 1 TEI production, 352 linked page images, three separately encoded King Horn versions, and the fully encoded distinct Horn Childe appendix.

evidence.citation: Computational element, milestone, and division inspection of repository acn1637.xml, checked against Hall's printed page layout.
evidence.locator: DIV1, L, LG, MILESTONE, NOTE1, and PB elements
evidence.summary: Counts 1,546 Harley lines, 1,569 Laud lines, 1,530 Cambridge lines, and 1,138 Horn Childe L elements, the last comprising 1,136 verse lines plus two ellipsis placeholders. The XML has 396 PB instances but 352 unique PB references because Harley and Laud share 44 print pages before serialization.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse, ACN1637.0001.001.
evidence.summary: Current public item for the U-M encoding and its rights statement.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Humanities Text Initiative, Middle English Compendium book-specific coding instructions for King Horn.
evidence.summary: Identifies ACN1637, the encoded King Horn and Horn Childe page ranges, the source title-page keying, and the decision not to preserve the print volume as an en-face electronic layout.

evidence.citation: Joseph Hall, ed., King Horn: A Middle-English Romance, Edited from the Manuscripts (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1901), Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: title page; introduction pp. vii-lvi; parallel texts pp. 1-89; Notes pp. 91-177; Horn Childe pp. 179-192
evidence.summary: Primary evidence for publication, Hall's direct three-witness method, transcription and correction policy, parallel layout, historical manuscript claims, distinct Horn Childe appendix, prior editions consulted, and exact supplied or corrected Horn Childe readings.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for kinghornmiddleen00hall.
evidence.summary: Records UC Libraries as contributor, 2008 upload metadata, and the available scan files; no item license URL is present.

evidence.citation: Current British Library, Bodleian Libraries, and Cambridge Digital Library records for the three King Horn witnesses.
evidence.summary: Supports current shelfmarks, folio ranges, dates, holdings, physical descriptions, and the distinction between manuscript facts and Hall's older hypotheses.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts, Harley MS 2253, item 58, Geste of Kyng Horn.

evidence.citation: Bodleian Libraries, Special Collections requests and copying services.
evidence.summary: Current routes for requesting physical special-collections material and mediated remote copying.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library TEI metadata for MS Gg.4.27(2).
evidence.locator: msItem 2; ff. 6r-13r; history, physical description, identifiers, and rights declarations
evidence.summary: Provides the first-quarter-fourteenth-century date, England origin, fourteen-leaf extent, one Anglicana scribe, two-column layout, exact King Horn item and folios, rebinding history, ARKs, and separate image-download and metadata rights statements.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library IIIF Presentation manifest for MS Gg.4.27(2).
evidence.summary: Enumerates the complete digital object, canvas labels, direct image services, King Horn range, and linked TEI metadata.

evidence.citation: Cambridge University Library, Visiting Special Collections and Ordering Images.
evidence.summary: Current physical consultation and imaging-order routes for Cambridge special collections.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland Archives and Manuscripts, Adv.MS.19.2.1 and digital record Adv.MS.19.2.1D.
evidence.locator: identifier; scope and contents; digital material; permanent digital link
evidence.summary: Confirms the current shelfmark, London 1331-1340 production, varied Middle English contents, six-scribe production, exact NLS digital-record identifier, and current permanent digital-object link.

evidence.citation: Auchinleck Manuscript project, Horn Childe and Maiden Rimnild.
evidence.locator: ff. 317va-323vb; Scribe 1; stanza form; lineation; facsimile links
evidence.summary: Establishes Horn Childe as a distinct, uniquely witnessed 1,136-line tail-rhyme romance in Advocates MS 19.2.1, ending imperfect with a lost leaf after fol. 321, and links the thirteen surviving photographed sides.

evidence.citation: Auchinleck Manuscript project, Physical make-up and History and owners.
evidence.summary: Describes the London c. 1331-1340 vellum manuscript, 331 folios and detached fragments, scribes, losses, historic shelfmark, and transfer to the National Library of Scotland.

evidence.citation: Auchinleck Manuscript project, home page, contents, and project history.
evidence.summary: Describes the full transcription and digital facsimile and enumerates manuscript items whose pages link 701 surviving leaf and fragment surfaces through public IIIF Image API endpoints.

evidence.citation: Auchinleck Manuscript project, How to refer to this site.
evidence.summary: States that project texts, essays, images, and descriptions are copyrighted, allows personal use, and requires authorization for commercial use or publication.

evidence.citation: National Library of Scotland, Consult collections in person and Copyright.
evidence.summary: Current NLS consultation procedures and general copyright guidance, distinct from the older project-specific reuse statement.

evidence.citation: Joseph Ritson, ed., Ancient Engleish Metrical Romanceës, vols. 2-3 (London, 1802), Internet Archive scans.
evidence.locator: King Horn, vol. 2, 91-155; Horn Childe, vol. 3, 282-320 and corrections
evidence.summary: Verifies Ritson's editions and the readings Hall attributes to him.

evidence.citation: Francisque Michel, ed., Horn et Rimenhild (Paris, 1845), Internet Archive scan.
evidence.locator: title page; Cambridge King Horn and variants; Horn Childe, 341-389
evidence.summary: Verifies Michel's edition and Hall's use of Michel's supplied inne at Horn Childe line 324.

evidence.citation: Theodor Wissmann, ed., Das Lied von King Horn, Quellen und Forschungen 45 (Strassburg, 1881), Internet Archive scan.
evidence.summary: Verifies the critical edition Hall explicitly acknowledges as especially helpful.

evidence.citation: Georg Caro, 'Kleine Publikationen aus der Auchinleck-Hs. X. Horn Childe and Maiden Rimnild,' Englische Studien 12 (1889), Internet Archive scan.
evidence.summary: Verifies Caro's Auchinleck edition and supplies adopted by Hall at Horn Childe lines 20 and 1081.

evidence.citation: Ronald B. Herzman, Graham Drake, and Eve Salisbury, eds., Four Romances of England (Kalamazoo: Medieval Institute Publications, 1999), current METS reading edition and PDF.
evidence.locator: title page; Introduction to King Horn; King Horn text and textual notes
evidence.summary: Identifies the 1999 edition, its Cambridge base text, selected emendations from Laud and Harley, and its status as later comparison scholarship.

evidence.citation: U.S. Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, and Circular 38B, Copyright Restoration Under the URAA, revised 2026.
evidence.locator: Circular 15A, pp. 2-3; Circular 38B, eligibility and Term of Copyright
evidence.summary: Provides the current U.S. duration framework and the foreign-work restoration rule. Eligible restored works receive only the remainder of the term they would have had; the normal pre-1978 maximum is 95 years from publication, so any possible restored terms in the 1802, 1845, 1881, 1889, and 1901 editions have expired.

open_questions.question: When will the complete Harley MS 2253 digital facsimile and its item-level reuse statement become publicly inspectable again?
next_steps: Recheck the exact British Library record and digitised-manuscripts service after restoration.
Cache the complete public codex if downloads are then permitted.
notes: The Harley codex cannot yet be cached or visually rechecked against Hall, and image-specific rights remain unknown.

open_questions.question: How should the current U-M No Copyright - United States label be reconciled with the older generic conditional availability notice embedded in the 2006 XML?
next_steps: Seek item-level clarification from U-M if a use depends on the difference.
notes: Users need both current and historical rights evidence rather than an unexplained single conclusion.

open_questions.question: Does CUDL intend its machine-readable metadata license target to remain CC BY-NC-ND 3.0 while the accompanying prose names CC BY-NC-ND 4.0?
next_steps: Ask CUDL for clarification before metadata redistribution that depends on the version.
notes: The version conflict affects reuse of catalog metadata but not the separately stated CC BY-NC 4.0 license for downloadable images.

open_questions.question: Can a secure textual genealogy among the three King Horn witnesses or among King Horn, Horn et Rimenhild, Horn Childe, and Hind Horn be established from current scholarship?
next_steps: Treat the works and witnesses as related without directional filiation.
Revisit only with dedicated textual-critical evidence.
notes: Hall's historical stemmatic and source suggestions should not be converted into directional lineage without stronger evidence.
